primary_decisions_supported: Resource allocation for growth vs retention
executive_questions_answered: What is our projected ARR in 3-5 years under current assumptions?
How do different growth scenarios (upside/base/downside) impact cash flow and valuation?
Are our targets realistic given historical performance?

metric_definitions.definition: Forecasted recurring revenue for future years, using current ARR plus new bookings minus churn for each year

known_variants.definition_delta: Projects ARR from bookings inputs (new & expansion) with assumed churn rates
known_variants.when_to_use: High-level planning when detailed retention data is unavailable

pitfalls: Overly optimistic assumptions lead to unrealistic projections
Ignoring ramp time or market saturation skews results
Mixing ARR with one-time revenue confuses recurring forecast
High growth rates are harder to sustain on a larger base
Growth% alone doesn't show quality (e.g., churn could be high but masked by new sales)
Changing fiscal year or period alignment can distort simple YoY calculations

joins_and_keys.notes: Link bookings to accounts for base ARR
Bring in current ARR and renewal data

transformation_rules: Annualize contract values for ARR consistency (e.g., multi-year deals to annual ARR)
Exclude non-recurring revenue from ARR calculations
Convert all amounts to a single currency (e.g., USD) for consolidation

data_quality_checks.logic: Verify Starting ARR + Net ARR Added = Ending ARR each year
Flag if scenario assumptions (growth%, churn%) exceed realistic bounds

derived_fields: New_ARR = sum(New logo bookings) * (portion starting in period)
aggregation_rules: Aggregate ARR projections by scenario and year
Sum bookings across all accounts for total New/Expansion ARR
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Driver-based assumptions (growth %, churn %, expansion %) applied to current ARR to forecast future ARR for each scenario

layout.purpose: Show projected ARR growth across scenarios to compare outcomes
Illustrate year-over-year ARR growth and its components

narrative_insights_to_generate: Compare upside vs base vs downside outcomes and their implications
Highlight which assumptions (e.g., churn, new sales) most impact the projections
diagnostic_questions: Which scenario assumptions are driving the largest differences in ARR outcomes?
How does the projected ARR in each scenario compare to our plan and what gaps emerge?

recommended_actions.action: Adjust hiring or spend in line with base case trajectory
Prepare contingency plans for downside scenario
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Leading indicators (pipeline, churn) trend toward downside case

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Basic model with total ARR and single growth/churn assumptions for all
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Granular model by segment/product with interactive scenario toggles and real-time actuals vs plan comparison
common_client_customizations: Additional scenarios (e.g., Target case beyond base)
privacy_and_access_notes: Limit detailed forecast access to Finance/Execs; share high-level figures more broadly
If using customer-level data for projections, aggregate or anonymize when presenting externally
success_criteria: Projection accuracy: first forecast year ARR within ~5% of actual
Model becomes source of truth for board planning sessions

references.title: Revenue forecasting in finance: A comprehensive guide for SaaS
